original: Fé vældr frænda róge; / fǿðesk úlfr í skóge.

translations.standard: Wealth causes the strife of kinsmen; / the wolf is fed in the forest.
translations.literal: Wealth causes kinsmen's strife; / feeds-itself wolf in wood.

original: Þurs vældr kvenna kvillu; / kátr værðr fár af illu.

translations.standard: Giant causes the sickness of women; / few grow cheerful from misfortune.
translations.literal: Giant causes women's sickness; / cheerful becomes few from ill [evil].

original: Óss er flæstra færða / för; en skálpr er sværða.

translations.standard: Estuary is the way of most journeys; / but a scabbard is that of swords.
translations.literal: Estuary is most journeys' / way; but scabbard is [of] swords.

original: Reið kveða hrossom væsta; / Reginn sló sverðit bæzta.

translations.standard: Riding is said to be the worst thing for horses; / Reginn forged the best sword.
translations.literal: Riding say [to] horses worst; / Reginn struck sword-the best.

original: Kaun er barna bölve; / böl gørver mann fölvan.

translations.standard: Ulcer is the bane of children; / misfortune makes a man pale.
translations.literal: Ulcer is children's bane; / bale makes man pale.

original: Hagall er kaldastr korna; / Kristr skóp hæimenn forna.

translations.standard: Hail is the coldest of grains; / Christ created the world of old.
translations.literal: Hail is coldest [of] grains; / Christ shaped world-the ancient.

original: Nauðr gerer næppa koste; / nøktan kælr í froste.

translations.standard: Constraint gives scant choice; / a naked man is chilled by the frost.
translations.literal: Need makes narrow choice [conditions]; / naked-one cools in frost.

original: Ís kǫllum brú bræiða; / blindan þarf at læiða.

translations.standard: Ice we call the broad bridge; / the blind man must be led.
translations.literal: Ice call-we bridge broad; / blind-man needs to lead [be led].

original: Ár er gumna góðe; / get ek at ǫrr var Fróðe.

translations.standard: Plenty is a boon to men; / I say that Frodi was generous.
translations.literal: Year [Harvest] is men's benefit; / guess I that generous was Frodi.

original: Sól er landa ljóme; / lúti ek helgum dóme.

translations.standard: Sun is the light of the lands; / I bow to the divine decree.
translations.literal: Sun is lands' light; / bow I [to] holy judgment.

original: Týr er einhendr áss / ok úlfs leifar / ok hofa hilmir.

translations.standard: Tyr is the one-handed god / and the leavings of the wolf / and the ruler of temples.
translations.literal: Tyr is one-handed god / and wolf's leavings / and temples' ruler.

original: Bjarkan er laufgrønstr líma; / Kristr skóp mǫrg tíma.

translations.standard: Birch is the greenest of leafy boughs; / Christ created many opportunities.
translations.literal: Birch is greenest [of] leaves; / Christ shaped many times [opportunities].

original: Maðr er moldar auki; / mikil er greip á hauki.

translations.standard: Man is the increase of dust; / great is the claw of the hawk.
translations.literal: Man is dust's increase; / great is grip on hawk.

original: Lǫgr er, er fellr ór fjalle / fǫrr; en gull ero nosse.

translations.standard: Water is that which falls from a mountain / as a stream; but gold is a precious thing.
translations.literal: Water is, which falls out-of mountain / journey [stream]; but gold are treasures.

original: Ýr er grønstr vetra; / vant er, er brennr, at létta.

translations.standard: Yew is the greenest of trees in winter; / it is wont to crackle when it burns.
translations.literal: Yew is greenest [of] winters; / wont is, when burns, to crackle.
